title: How do I connect to NUwave on Chromebook?
plain_text: NUwave is one of Northeastern University's secure wireless networks available at the Boston campus for instructors, staff, and students. Connect to NUwave on Chromebook 1. Select the Wi-Fi icon located on the bottom-right taskbar. 2. Choose NUwave or eduroam. 3. Ensure that the correct authentication methods are selected. 4. Select PEAP under 'EAP method'. 5. Select MSCHAPV2 under 'Phase 2 authentication'. 6. Choose Default under 'CA certificate'. 7. Leave the 'Anonymous Identity' field blank. 8. Type your Northeastern username under Identity. 9. Type your Northeastern password under Password.
body_html:
NUwave is one of Northeastern University's secure wireless networks available at the Boston campus for instructors, staff, and students.

Connect to NUwave on Chromebook

  • Select the Wi-Fi icon located on the bottom-right taskbar.
  • Choose NUwave or eduroam.
  • Ensure that the correct authentication methods are selected.
  • Select PEAP under 'EAP method'.
  • Select MSCHAPV2 under 'Phase 2 authentication'.
  • Choose Default under 'CA certificate'.
  • Leave the 'Anonymous Identity' field blank.
  • Type your Northeastern username under Identity.
  • Type your Northeastern password under Password.

steps: Select the Wi-Fi icon located on the bottom-right taskbar.
